body:
closes #283 - This PR adds the options to use different selectors such as `MatchValue` and `Index` instead of just `NearestValue`. 

`MatchValue` is needed when you need to access a particular with error checking. For example, in calibration, it is better to error when the date does not not exists than grab a date that is close enough. `Index` is helpful if you want to access the data by index instead. The other reason is that the dimension is not necessarily one dimensional, so this gives a way out of that situation.

**TODO**

- [x] Add tests for error handling
- [x] Add documentation (howdoi.md => add question about slicing with a dimension that is 2d)
- [x] Move slice and window to the new file
title: Add selectors for slicing and windowing
